entities.name: antara-jñātā
entities.cite: The subject now is the antarah jnata, the inner knower

entities.name: vāg-ātman
entities.cite: already standing suksme vagatmani, in a subtle speech-self — language is not something the knower produces but a dimension it inhabits

entities.name: tejas
entities.cite: is ripened tejasa — by the inner heat, the old physiological fire of the Upanisads

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: the antarah jnata, the inner knower, already standing suksme vagatmani, in a subtle speech-self

axioms.name: vivartate
axioms.cite: the verb is vivartate (112d): it *appears as* wordhood, the same appearance-without-transformation that 1.1 predicated of Brahman

axioms.name: apadyate
axioms.cite: The word-nature attains mano-bhava (mind-condition), is ripened tejasa — by the inner heat, the old physiological fire of the Upanisads

axioms.name: paka
axioms.cite: is ripened tejasa — by the inner heat

denials.name: parinamate
denials.cite: the same appearance-without-transformation that 1.1 predicated of Brahman and 1.48 of the sphota

claims.name: vivartate
claims.cite: turns itself, for the manifestation of its own form, into wordhood

rejected_readings.label: actual_transformation
rejected_readings.rendering: The inner knower actually transforms into wordhood as a real alteration of substance.
rejected_readings.why: Asserts parinamate (real transformation) rather than vivarta (appearance-without-transformation), which the commentary explicitly contrasts with the vivarta doctrine of 1.1 and 1.48.

rejected_readings.label: speech_as_instrument
rejected_readings.rendering: The knower produces wordhood as an external tool for communication.
rejected_readings.why: Treats speech as something the knower makes rather than a dimension it inhabits. The commentary is explicit: 'language is not something the knower produces but a dimension it inhabits', making this a fundamental ontological category error.

rejected_readings.label: direct_utterance
rejected_readings.rendering: The knower-as-wordhood directly becomes utterance without intermediate mental or physiological stages.
rejected_readings.why: Omits the mandatory three-stage descent — knower's subtle speech → mental word → breath-borne utterance — which the verse explicitly elaborates. The commentary identifies this as 'the embryo of the pasyanti/madhyama/vaikhari hierarchy made explicit at 1.142'.

notes: Verse 1.112-113 formalizes the descent-mechanism connecting idealist metaphysics (knower in speech-essence via vivarta) to pneumatic physiology (mind→fire→breath→utterance). The cascade of relations encodes what the commentary calls the 'three-stage descent — knower's subtle speech, mental word, breath-borne utterance' that anticipates the explicit pasyanti/madhyama/vaikhari taxonomy. Fire (tejas) appears as a ripening agent, integrating the Upanisadic pneumatic framework into Bhartrhari's epistemology.
